candidateFindings.shortQuote: Maimonides uses VSLO to receive visiting student applications from U.S. LCME and Osteopathic accredited schools, while affiliated foreign medical school students should use the Maimonides online application system.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Internships and Undergraduate Medical Education | Maimonides Health

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: International observership lane is 'Only for affiliated schools' verbatim — not an open IMG pathway despite Maimonides being a Brooklyn safety-net hospital.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Affiliated-foreign-school list not published; cost and visa absent.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) US LCME/COCA via VSLO is the primary public lane; (b) international observership is AFFILIATED_ONLY (specific home-school pre-affiliation required) — must NOT be claimed as open IMG-friendly; (c) cost/visa NOT_STATED.
plainEnglishSummary: Maimonides Medical Center accepts visiting fourth-year medical students from US LCME or osteopathic schools via VSLO. International medical students have a separate lane but it is ONLY for affiliated foreign medical schools — there is no open IMG observership at Maimonides despite its Brooklyn safety-net status. Cost and visa not stated on the hub.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Brooklyn safety-net Maimonides was correctly classified as VSLO-US-only with affiliation-only intl — protects against the common 'Brooklyn safety-net = IMG-friendly' overclaim pattern.
